🏃 Hiking Day 3 Directions

Hosshinmon-oji → Kumano Hongu Taisha → Yunomine Onsen • Saturday, May 23, 2026
Matthew Wong & Mary Siu • Kumano Travel Res #186433
🚌 Transfer — Bus to Trailhead
Bus — Minshuku Chikatsuyu → Hosshinmon-oji Sat May 23 • Ryujin Bus — Kumano Hongu Line • Direct, no transfer
Transfer
🚌 Ryujin Bus — Chikatsuyu-oji Direct Depart Chikatsuyu-oji at 12:49 on the Ryujin Bus Kumano Hongu Line. No transfer required — alight directly at Hosshinmon-oji. Enjoy a relaxed morning at the minshuku before departure.
⏱ Leave Minshuku by 12:44 • ~5 min walk to stop • Est. arrive Hosshinmon-oji ~13:15
🚶 Boarding Stop — Chikatsuyu-oji (近露王子) • Ryujin Bus Walk east along the main village road from Minshuku Chikatsuyu — a short 5-minute stroll.
  • Exit the Minshuku and turn right (east) onto the main village road
  • Walk ~5 min along the flat paved road
  • The stop is near the Chikatsuyu-oji shrine stone monument — look for the roadside bus pole
  • Bus stop sign reads 近露王子 (Chikatsuyu-oji)
  • Board toward Hongu / Hosshinmon-oji direction (not toward Kii-Tanabe)
☁️ Hail-and-ride boarding available — signal the driver from the roadside if needed.
🚌 Direct Route — No Transfer
Ryujin Bus: Chikatsuyu-oji 12:49 → Hosshinmon-oji ~13:15 (direct, no change needed)
Alight at: Hosshinmon-oji — bus stop is directly at the oji shrine
Collect stamp at the Hosshinmon-oji stamp box (20 m from bus stop) before starting the trail
✅ Kumano Sanzan bus tour on Day 4 covers Hongu Taisha again — no need to rush the arrival today.
💲 No IC Cards — Exact Cash Required No Suica, Pasmo or IC cards accepted on Ryujin rural buses. Prepare exact coins and small bills before boarding. Confirm fare with driver on boarding.
📦 Luggage — Leave at Minshuku Chikatsuyu by 9:30 AM Maruo luggage shuttle will collect 2 bags from the accommodation and deliver directly to Minshuku Takiyoshi in the Hongu area. Do not carry bags on the trail.
🏃 Trail — Hosshinmon-oji to Kumano Hongu Taisha
Hosshinmon-oji → Kumano Hongu Taisha Nakahechi Route • Final Section • Sat May 23
Hike
7.5 kmDistance
2–3 hrsEst. Time
+190m / −460mElevation
2 / 5Difficulty
⏱ Timing Guide — This section is predominantly downhill through cedar forest and village roads. Allow extra time to visit Oyunohara (+30–45 min) after reaching Hongu Taisha. Check-in at Minshuku Takiyoshi is 15:00–17:00.
Est. arrive Hongu Taisha ~15:15–15:45 Target bus to Yunomine: 16:40 dep. Hongu Arrive Yunomine Onsen: ~17:04 Check-in: 15:00–17:00
📍 Trail Navigation This is one of the best-marked sections of the entire Kumano Kodo:
  • Stone/wooden distance markers every 500m counting down to Kumano Hongu Taisha
  • Oji shrine signs at each waypoint with name, history, and mini-map
  • From Hosshinmon-oji: follow the paved road through Hosshinmon Village (~1.5 km) until the trail re-enters the forest at Mizunomi-oji
  • After Mizunomi-oji: forest trail descends continuously — follow the clear dirt path south
  • At Sangen-jaya junction: stay on the main Nakahechi trail (right/south); the Kohechi Route branches left toward Koyasan — do not take it
  • Look for the optional lookout side trail near signpost marker 73
📲 Offline Maps Gaia GPS • Maps.me • Wikiloc • tb-kumano.jp PDF (download before departure)
Stamp = Nakahechi stamp box (red ink) • 4 stamps on this section (Hosshinmon, Mizunomi, Fushiogami, Haraido)
  • 1
    Hosshinmon-oji 発心門王子 Stamp
    Trailhead • Bus Stop — "Gate of Awakening of the Aspiration to Enlightenment." One of the five great oji shrines of the Nakahechi, marking the outermost sacred boundary of Kumano Hongu Taisha. Medieval pilgrims believed crossing this gate was a symbolic death and rebirth into the Pure Land.

    The Ryujin Bus stop is directly at the oji — the stamp box is just 20 meters away as you exit the bus. Collect your stamp before setting off.

    From here, follow the paved road south through Hosshinmon Village (~1.5 km). Watch for roadside folk-art wood carvings for sale on an honor-system. Keep the shrine on your right as you exit the bus stop area.
    🐛 Leeches — Apply Yamabiru-fai Before Setting Off Before entering the trail, apply Yamabiru-fai leech repellent to shoes, socks, and lower legs. The cedar forest between Hosshinmon-oji and Mizunomi-oji has damp, shaded conditions where yamabiru (land leeches) are active May–October. Reapply at each rest stop.
    Hosshinmon-oji — CC BY-SA 3.0, Wikimedia Commons (KMR)
  • 2
    Mizunomi-oji 水飲王子 Stamp
    ~1.5 km from start • Forest trail begins here — "Water-drinking Shrine," named for a spring that refreshed thirsty pilgrims. The paved road section ends here and the trail enters the forest. Look for the 1723 chlorite stone monument erected by the Kishu domain lord, and a horizontally-split Jizo statue where pilgrims place coins to pray for relief from backache — apt after days of mountain walking.
  • 3
    Fushiogami-oji 伏拝王子 Stamp
    ~4.5 km from start • Key viewpoint — "Shrine of Prostrate Worship." The most emotionally charged stop on this section — the spot where medieval pilgrims, after days of mountain walking, first saw Kumano Hongu Taisha far below in the valley and fell to their knees. A stone monument commemorates the Heian Period poetess Izumi Shikibu, who made this pilgrimage.

    Look for the panoramic viewpoint toward the valley (Hongu Taisha visible as a white dot below). A seasonal teahouse offers coffee made with hot spring water (¥200) and handmade snacks — check if open.
    🚽 Restroom Available — Fushiogami-oji (confirmed, well-maintained)
    Fushiogami-oji — CC BY-SA 3.0, Wikimedia Commons (KMR)
  • 4
    Sangen-jaya 三軒茶屋
    Historic rest area • Route junction — "Three-House Tea Shop," a centuries-old resting place. A stone tablet marks the Kohechi Route junction here — the 78 km trail to Koyasan branches to the left. Stay on the main Nakahechi trail (continue straight/right toward Kumano Hongu Taisha). The trail begins its final descent through forest from here.
  • 5
    Valley Lookout Point
    Side trail • Near signpost marker 73 • "Not to be missed" (tb-kumano.jp) — A short detour off the main path reveals a sweeping view of the Kumano-gawa River below and the giant Oyunohara torii gate (33.9m tall) rising from the former shrine site. The scale of the torii from above is stunning. Return to the main trail and continue descending.
  • 6
    Haraido-oji 祓戸王子 Stamp
    Last oji before Hongu Taisha — "Purification Shrine." Pilgrims performed ritual cleansing here before entering the main sanctuary — the final spiritual preparation after the long journey. Located just beyond a torii arch, on the left side behind the main shrine precinct. A large Quercus oak tree marks the spot.
  • 7
    Kumano Hongu Taisha 熊野本宮大社 Stamp
    Final destination • UNESCO World Heritage — Head shrine of over 3,000 Kumano shrines across Japan, and the spiritual terminus of all Kumano Kodo routes. Built with traditional cypress bark roofing and joinery construction (minimal nails). The Yatagarasu (three-legged sacred crow) symbol appears throughout — the crow that guided the first emperor to Yamato.

    Hikers arrive via the rear/forest entrance. The famous long stone staircase (main approach) is on the front/road side — worth descending for the full view.

    Shrine hours: Grounds open daily; Shrine office 8:00–17:00. Treasure Hall 9:00–16:00 (adults ¥300). Restrooms available at shrine complex.
    🚽 Restroom Available — Kumano Hongu Taisha shrine complex
    Kumano Hongu Taisha — CC BY-SA 3.0, Wikimedia Commons (Yanajin33)
  • 8
    Oyunohara 大斎原 — Optional Side Trip
    ~10 min walk from Hongu Taisha (±500m) — The original location of Kumano Hongu Taisha, at the sacred confluence of the Kumano and Otonashi rivers. A catastrophic flood in 1889 destroyed most buildings and the shrine was relocated to higher ground. Eight deities remain enshrined in stone monuments on the former platform.

    The Otorii gate (erected 2000) is the world's largest torii — 33.9m tall, 42m wide, 172 tons. The scale is impossible to grasp until you stand beneath it. Highly recommended — allow 30–45 minutes round-trip. A separate Oyunohara goshuin is available at the Hongu Taisha shrine office.
    Oyunohara giant torii (33.9m) — CC BY 4.0, Wikimedia Commons (Douglas Perkins)
🚌 Transfer — Hongu Taisha to Yunomine Onsen
Ryujin Bus — Hongu Taisha-mae → Yunomine Onsen Multiple departures throughout the day • ~24 min ride
Transfer
Hongu Taisha-mae → Yunomine Onsen
📍 Boarding — Hongu Taisha-mae Bus Stop The bus stop is directly in front of the Kumano Hongu Heritage Center (世界遺産熊野本宮館) — the large wooden building on the road below the shrine approach staircase. Board at the roadside pole stop in front of the building.
Afternoon Ryujin Bus Schedule — Hongu Taisha-mae → Yunomine Onsen
Depart Hongu Arrive Yunomine Notes
15:05 15:29 Possible only with fast hiking and no extended stops
16:40 ⭐ 17:04 Your realistic target from 13:15 trailhead start — call Takiyoshi in advance (0735-42-1515)
⚠️ No further service to Yunomine Onsen after 16:40. All buses run daily including Saturdays.

Fare: ¥310–¥600 per person (¥620–¥1,200 total). Confirm exact fare to Yunomine Onsen at the stop board. Pay on exit.

💲 No IC Cards — Exact Cash Required No Suica, Pasmo or IC cards accepted. Prepare exact change before boarding.
🚗 Taxi Alternative — A taxi from Hongu Taisha-mae to Minshuku Takiyoshi (~8–10 km, ~15–20 min) costs approximately ¥3,000–5,000 and removes the 16:40 bus deadline, allowing more time at Hongu Taisha or Oyunohara. Must be pre-booked — taxis cannot be hailed on the spot in this area. Ask the Heritage Center staff or shrine office to call a local taxi, or arrange in advance through Kumano Travel (Res #186433).
Yunomine Onsen village — CC BY-SA 3.0, Wikimedia Commons (YOSHIFUMI OGISO)
🚶 Walk to Minshuku Takiyoshi — ~2 min (80m) Minshuku Takiyoshi is the closest accommodation to the bus stop — just 80 meters south along the single village road.
  • Exit the bus and walk south along the main village road
  • Minshuku Takiyoshi is on your right after ~80m, before reaching the Tsuboyu bath area
  • One road through the valley — impossible to miss
♨ Yunomine Onsen is one of Japan's oldest hot spring villages, in continuous use for over 1,800 years. The historic Tsuboyu bath (a small river-cave bathhouse) is the world's only UNESCO World Heritage-registered hot spring you can actually bathe in. Reserve your 30-minute slot at the Yunomine tourist hut upon arrival.
🏠 Accommodation — Minshuku Takiyoshi
Minshuku Takiyoshi Hongu Area • Yunomine Onsen • Sat May 23
Hotel

Check-in: 15:00–17:00. If arriving after 17:00, contact hotel in advance.

Meals: Supper & Breakfast included. Dinner served at 18:00 or 18:30 — first-come, first-served seating.

📦 Luggage Delivery 2 bags collected from Minshuku Chikatsuyu (leave by 9:30 AM) and delivered directly to Minshuku Takiyoshi via Maruo shuttle. Bags will be at the accommodation upon your arrival.
Minshuku Takiyoshi (滝よし) — Traditional inn, Yunomine Onsen
📞 TEL: 0735-42-1515 • Kumano Travel Res #186433 • Contact if arriving after 17:00
